evidence: "The agreement, valued in the range of $7 billion to $9 billion (AED25.7 to AED33 billion) over its 14-year term" for "up to 1.2 million metric tonnes per annum (mmtpa) of liquefied natural gas (LNG) to India" (ADNOC Gas press release, 2023, IOCL)

"14-year sales and purchase agreement (SPA) with Indian Oil Corporation Ltd (IndianOil) for the export of up to 1.2 million tonnes per annum (mtpa)" ... "with first deliveries to begin in 2026" (ADNOC Gas press release, Feb 2025)

"10-year agreement to supply 0.5 million metric tons per annum (mmtpa) of liquified natural gas (LNG) to GAIL India Limited"; CEO: "India continues to be a key market for ADNOC Gas, and this latest supply agreement underscores our ongoing dedication to fostering long-term partnerships" (ADNOC Gas press release, Nov 2024)

CEO on HPCL SPA: "This long-term agreement with HPCL, our third with Indian companies in the past year, reflects the robust energy partnership between the UAE and India" (ADNOC Gas press release, 2025)
CEO: "ADNOC Gas looks forward to supporting India's plans to make gas 15% of its primary energy basket by 2030" (ADNOC Gas press release, Feb 2025)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Three signed long-term India LNG SPAs: IOCL 14y up to 1.2 mtpa ($7-9bn), GAIL 10y 0.5 mmtpa, HPCL 10y 0.5 mmtpa - deliveries from 2026 ex Das Island
india_apac_interest.report_used: ADNOC Gas FY2024 results press release (Feb 2025) + official ADNOC Gas press releases 2023-2025

companies.notes: UNVERIFIED (yfinance gap): ADX .AE tickers 404 on yfinance. Figures from FY2024 results release: revenue $24.43bn (+7%), record net income $5.0bn (margin ~20.5%); ROE not computed (equity not captured).

evidence: "India's growth was particularly notable, driven by infrastructure expansion, while ASEAN demand increased due to reshoring in consumer electronics, textiles, footwear, machinery and electrical industries. This, combined with a rising middle-class population, represents an exciting growth opportunity for Borouge." (Borouge Annual Report 2024)
"Asia Pacific accounted for 63% of total sales volumes in 2024, while the Middle East and Africa contributed 30%" (Borouge Annual Report 2024)
Revenue by customer location: India $818.8m in 2024 - second-largest country market after China $1,803.7m (Borouge Annual Report 2024, Note 18)

"Through its HE3490-LS drainage system, Borouge was able to meet the demands of a highly challenging project at the Kakrapar Atomic Power Plant in Gujarat, India" ... "contributing to India's growing energy infrastructure" (Borouge Annual Report 2024)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Sales/marketing offices in New Delhi and Mumbai; India = 13.6% of FY2024 revenue (2nd-largest country market); no India manufacturing capex commitment in AR

companies.notes: UNVERIFIED (yfinance gap): ADX not on yfinance. AR2024: revenue $6,026.1m (+4.1%), net profit $1.24bn (+24%, margin ~20.6%), equity $4,482.1m (ROE ~27.7%). Borouge Group International merger (Borealis+Borouge+Nova, announced Mar 2025) will restructure the listed entity in 2026.

evidence: "...access to key end markets (including Brazil, India, and East Africa), freight and logistics optimization..." (Fertiglobe Annual Report 2024, commercial synergies of ADNOC control)

"In November 2023, Fertiglobe announced its first shipment of ISCC PLUS-certified renewable ammonia from its facilities in Egypt for use in the production of near-zero emissions soda ash, a key ingredient in laundry powder, to be manufactured by Unilever in India." (Fertiglobe Annual Report 2024)
Revenue by destination FY2024: Asia & Oceania $709.4m (35%) - largest region alongside Europe $720.8m (Fertiglobe Annual Report 2024)

companies.notes: UNVERIFIED (yfinance gap): ADX not on yfinance. AR2024: revenue $2,009m (~-17%), net profit attributable $160m (-54%, margin ~8.0%), equity $1,537.7m (ROE ~10.4% incl. NCI). ADNOC owns 86.2%.

evidence: Hetero is "one of India's leading generic pharmaceutical companies and the world's largest producer of anti-retroviral drugs"; "Julphar will distribute and commercialize several products in multiple therapeutic areas such as cardiovascular and nervous system (NS) and pain management." (Julphar press release, 2022 - Indian products into MENA, not Julphar investing in India)

"In 2025, Julphar announced an exclusive licensing partnership with Dong-A ST to tech transfer, manufacture, and commercialize a Darbepoetin Alfa biosimilar... across the MENA region." (Julphar Integrated Report 2025)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No India operations; inbound licensing from India's Hetero (2022) and Korea's Dong-A ST (2025), plus Julphar Bangladesh Ltd plant (Dhaka, since 2009). Growth capex points at Saudi Arabia (AED 300m biologics facility), not APAC.

"seasonal demand from major markets such as India and Brazil" (Industries Qatar Annual Report 2025)
